params.name: part
params.description: Specifies a comma-separated list of one or more resource properties that the API response will include. The part parameter value must include the id property.

params.name: id
params.description: Comma-separated list of YouTube broadcast IDs for the resources being retrieved.

params.name: mine
params.description: Set this parameter to true to instruct the API to return only broadcasts owned by the authenticated user.

params.name: broadcastStatus
params.description: Filters the API response to only include broadcasts with the specified status.

params.name: broadcastType
params.description: Filters results by the type of broadcast. Acceptable values are event and persistent.

params.name: maxResults
params.description: The maximum number of items that should be returned in the result set. Acceptable values are 0 to 50, inclusive.

params.name: pageToken
params.description: Identifies a specific page in the result set that should be returned. The nextPageToken property in the API response identifies other pages that are available.

params.name: fields
params.description: Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response. Use this parameter to reduce bandwidth usage by selecting only the fields you need.

params.name: key
params.description: API key. Your API key identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.

items.docs: Returns a list of YouTube broadcasts that match the API request parameters. You can retrieve broadcasts for the authenticated user or retrieve specific broadcasts by their IDs.

items.docs: Creates a broadcast. The authenticated user must be authorized to create broadcasts. After creating the broadcast, you will need to bind it to a stream and set it live.

items.docs: Updates a broadcast's settings. The authenticated user must own the broadcast being updated. Note that certain properties cannot be modified after a broadcast goes live.

params.name: id
params.description: The ID of the broadcast to delete.

params.name: key
params.description: API key. Your API key identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.

items.docs: Deletes a broadcast. The authenticated user must own the broadcast being deleted. Broadcasts cannot be deleted while they are in the live or testing status.

params.name: id
params.description: The id parameter specifies the unique ID of the broadcast that is being bound to a video stream.

params.name: part
params.description: Specifies a comma-separated list of one or more resource properties that the API response will include. The part parameter value must include the id property.

params.name: streamId
params.description: The streamId parameter specifies the unique ID of the video stream that is being bound to a broadcast. If this parameter is omitted, the API will remove any existing binding between the broadcast and a video stream.

params.name: key
params.description: API key. Your API key identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.

items.docs: Binds a YouTube broadcast to a stream or removes an existing binding between a broadcast and a stream. A broadcast can only be bound to one video stream, though a video stream may be bound to more than one broadcast.

params.name: id
params.description: The id parameter specifies the unique ID of the broadcast that is transitioning to another status.

params.name: broadcastStatus
params.description: The broadcastStatus parameter identifies the state to which the broadcast is changing.

params.name: part
params.description: Specifies a comma-separated list of one or more resource properties that the API response will include. The part parameter value must include the id property.

params.name: key
params.description: API key. Your API key identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.

items.docs: Changes the status of a YouTube live broadcast and initiates any processes associated with the new status. Valid transitions are: ready -> testing, testing -> live, live -> complete, ready -> live (skips testing state).

params.name: liveChatId
params.description: The liveChatId parameter specifies the ID of the live chat whose messages will be returned. The live chat ID associated with a broadcast is returned in the liveBroadcast resource's snippet.liveChatId property.

items.docs: Lists live chat messages for a specific live broadcast. This method does not support filtering by user. The list is ordered chronologically, starting with the earliest message.
